page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the N1 machine family. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 86.0 GB of dedicated system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 0.9 GB per core. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests falls into the lower tier, the massive core count enables strong multi-core performance, placing it in the top tier for multi-core stress-ng benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read and write operations registering strong throughput in both cached and uncached block tests. Additionally, the instance demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, alongside average capabilities in software compilation and ray tracing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highcpu-96 offers a dense allocation of compute resources without the added expense of excess memory or local storage. This resource balance makes it highly suitable for parallelized, compute-bound workloads such as batch processing, static web serving, and multi-threaded compilation. However, memory-intensive applications or tasks requiring high single-threaded performance may encounter bottlenecks due to the low per-core memory allocation and modest single-core benchmark results.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs and 86.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor with 48 physical cores, this instance lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. While single-core benchmark results are modest, the server delivers strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth. It excels in highly parallelized tasks, showing strong throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 0.9 GB per core makes it a cost-efficient choice for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints, such as multi-threaded software compilation, batch processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 86 GB RAM. It delivers strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 86 GB RAM, optimized for parallel compute and high-throughput web serving.
tagline: A high-core Intel Xeon virtual machine optimized for parallelized compute workloads, high-throughput web serving, and memory-bandwidth-intensive tasks.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon processors
86.0 GB of system memory with high read and write bandwidth
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput static web serving
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for parallelized batch processing and multi-threaded compilation
